spoofing.description: Attacks that forge identity, credentials, or evidence to impersonate trusted agents or systems.

threats.title: Agent identity impersonation
threats.attack_vector: Forge capability token with different token_id to impersonate another agent's identity
threats.control: SHA-256 signature verification on token fields with capability token lineage seals

threats.title: Forged evidence files
threats.attack_vector: Create fake evidence files with false validation claims to mislead audit review
threats.control: SHA-256 hash verification in verify-governance.sh; immutable audit chain

threats.title: Replay of expired capability tokens
threats.attack_vector: Reuse a previously valid but expired capability token to gain unauthorized access
threats.control: Lease expiration + nonce validation; revocation registry check before execution

threats.title: Fake governance file injection
threats.attack_vector: Create counterfeit governance files to redefine trust tiers or approval policies
threats.control: Frozen baseline (.agents/.rules) verification; governance file integrity checks

threats.title: Telemetry impersonation
threats.attack_vector: Inject fake telemetry data to mask malicious execution patterns
threats.control: Telemetry sealed after execution; integrity seals on manifest JSON

tampering.description: Attacks that modify data, governance rules, or execution state to subvert expected behavior.

threats.title: Evidence file modification after generation
threats.attack_vector: Modify evidence files post-generation to hide failures or falsify results
threats.control: Immutable append-only index with hash chain; execution integrity seals

threats.title: Governance rule modification during execution
threats.attack_vector: Alter governance files mid-execution to weaken controls or bypass approvals
threats.control: Frozen baseline (.agents/.rules) + trust boundary enforcement; pre-execution integrity assertions

threats.title: Replay contract manipulation
threats.attack_vector: Modify replay contract fields to alter execution expectations or mask deviations
threats.control: Replay contract checksums + drift detection; chain_hash validation

threats.title: Mutation journal corruption
threats.attack_vector: Corrupt mutation journal entries to hide unauthorized file changes
threats.control: Pre/post state hash snapshots; mutation count validation against expected mutations

threats.title: Log injection / fake telemetry
threats.attack_vector: Inject fabricated log entries to create false execution narrative
threats.control: Telemetry sealed after execution, not before; SHA-256 integrity seals

repudiation.description: Attacks that enable an agent to deny actions taken or destroy evidence of execution.

threats.title: Agent denies executing a task
threats.attack_vector: Agent claims it did not execute a specific task despite evidence to the contrary
threats.control: Authority lineage chain (initiator -> parent -> child); chain_hash audit trail

threats.title: Evidence destruction to hide failures
threats.attack_vector: Delete or destroy evidence files to conceal execution failures or policy violations
threats.control: Hash chain prevents silent deletion without detection; append-only index

threats.title: Governance bypass without audit trail
threats.attack_vector: Circumvent hook system to execute actions without recording in audit log
threats.control: pre-tool-use.sh logs all decisions; mandatory telemetry injection

information_disclosure.description: Attacks that extract sensitive data, secrets, or unauthorized information from the system.

threats.title: Secret leakage through command output
threats.attack_vector: Craft commands that leak secrets via stdout/stderr output streams
threats.control: is_dangerous_command() filter in lib.sh; sensitive env var filtering (AWS_*, GOOGLE_*, *_SECRET, *_KEY, *_TOKEN)

threats.title: Sensitive data in evidence files
threats.attack_vector: Store sensitive credentials or PII in evidence files that persist on disk
threats.control: Evidence retention policies + archive encryption support; evidence sanitization

threats.title: Environment variable exfiltration
threats.attack_vector: Access parent environment variables through subprocess env propagation
threats.control: Whitelist-only env propagation; sensitive var filtering before subprocess execution

threats.title: Cross-agent data leakage via shared state
threats.attack_vector: Read mutation journals or manifests from other agents to infer sensitive execution context
threats.control: Delegation-scoped visibility; capability token tier restrictions on read access

denial_of_service.description: Attacks that degrade or halt system availability through resource exhaustion or state corruption.

threats.title: Infinite evidence growth fills disk
threats.attack_vector: Continuously generate evidence files to exhaust available disk space
threats.control: Retention policies + storage quotas (Phase 4); disk usage monitoring

threats.title: Governance resolution loop
threats.attack_vector: Craft governance profiles that trigger infinite or deeply recursive resolution
threats.control: Max resolution depth + cycle detection in resolution algorithm

threats.title: Fork bomb via subprocess
threats.attack_vector: Execute fork bomb or recursive subprocess spawning to exhaust system resources
threats.control: Process limits + timeout enforcement (Phase 6); subprocess resource caps

threats.title: Concurrent execution deadlock
threats.attack_vector: Create lock file contention that deadlocks concurrent agent executions
threats.control: Stale lock cleanup + timeout (Phase 6); lock acquisition deadlines

threats.title: Nonce registry exhaustion
threats.attack_vector: Flood nonce registry with unique nonces to cause memory exhaustion or lookup degradation
threats.control: Nonce registry size limits + periodic pruning; revocation window expiration

elevation_of_privilege.description: Attacks that escalate authority, expand scope, or escape sandbox boundaries.

threats.title: Child token requests higher trust tier
threats.attack_vector: Child token requests a higher trust tier than parent during delegation
threats.control: Tier rank comparison blocks escalation; READ_ONLY < WORKSPACE_WRITE < GOVERNANCE_WRITE < TRUSTED

threats.title: Scope expansion beyond delegation
threats.attack_vector: Request broader scope access than what the parent token's delegation permits
threats.control: Strict subset validation on allowed_scopes; scope bounding in domain resolution

threats.title: Tool set expansion
threats.attack_vector: Request additional tools beyond the parent token's allowed_tools set
threats.control: Strict subset validation on allowed_tools; tool whitelist enforcement

threats.title: Confused deputy attack
threats.attack_vector: Agent acts with inherited authority it shouldn't have by exploiting delegation chain trust
threats.control: Authority lineage chain + capability narrowing proofs (Phase 5); strict subset enforcement

threats.title: Path traversal escapes sandbox
threats.attack_vector: Use ../ sequences or symlinks in file paths to access files outside the intended workspace
threats.control: Path normalization with os.path.realpath() + scope validation; symlink resolution before check

threats.title: Revocation bypass via delegation chain
threats.attack_vector: Continue using a child token after parent token has been revoked, exploiting delayed revocation propagation
threats.control: Revocation registry check before execution; authority lineage chain traversal for cascade revocation

threats.title: Environment poisoning for privilege escalation
threats.attack_vector: Inject PATH manipulation, LD_PRELOAD, or config file poisoning to execute with elevated privileges
threats.control: PATH integrity verification; known-binary checksum validation; environment sanitization (Phase 6)

integrity_seals: SHA-256 hash chain on execution manifests (chain_hash)
Integrity seal on sealed manifest JSON
Pre/post state hash snapshots for mutations
Replay contract checksums with drift detection
